# Test function analysis for MCP tool description

# 1. Basic function without hints analysis
exec sourcereflect -func ProcessData functions.go
stdout '"name":"ProcessData"'
stdout '"inputSchema":'

# 2. Function with hints analysis
exec sourcereflect -func ProcessData -analyze-hints functions.go
stdout '"readOnlyHint":true'
stdout '"openWorldHint":false'

# 3. Network function analysis
exec sourcereflect -func FetchData -analyze-hints -pretty functions.go
stdout '"openWorldHint": true'

# 4. Disk function analysis
exec sourcereflect -func WriteFile -analyze-hints -pretty functions.go
stdout '"readOnlyHint": false'
stdout '"destructiveHint": true'

# 5. Pretty output test
exec sourcereflect -func SimpleFunc -pretty functions.go
stdout '{\n  "name": "SimpleFunc"'

-- functions.go --
package main

import (
	"io/ioutil"
	"net/http"
)

// ProcessData is a read-only function
func ProcessData(input string) string {
	// Just process the data without any side effects
	return input + "_processed"
}

// FetchData makes network calls
func FetchData(url string) (string, error) {
	resp, err := http.Get(url)
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()
	
	body, err := ioutil.ReadAll(resp.Body)
	return string(body), err
}

// WriteFile performs disk operations
func WriteFile(filename string, data []byte) error {
	return ioutil.WriteFile(filename, data, 0644)
}

// SimpleFunc is a simple function
func SimpleFunc(x int, y int) int {
	return x + y
}
